_README: This file holds ALL the words shown to participants. You can edit any text
below and the change appears in the app after you save this file and refresh
the browser. No coding and no rebuild are needed.
1. Only change the text INSIDE the double quotes, for example the part after
2. Keep every quote, comma, colon and curly brace exactly where it is. If the
page goes blank after an edit, you most likely deleted a quote or comma.
3. To make a word bold, wrap it in **double stars**. For italic, use *single
stars*. Example: "You will read **16 emails**, one at a time."
4. Words inside curly braces such as {role}, {name} or {total} are filled in
automatically by the app. Leave the braces and the word inside them as is.
5. Lines starting with an underscore, like this _README, are notes for you
and are never shown to participants.
Compensation and timing live in instructions.compensation below.
Job areas are stored with readable names, so they show as-is. Optional
on-screen renames live in clusterLabels. Each assigned role's title and
description live in the roles section, keyed by the job area name.

clusterLabels._note: Optional display overrides. Left side = the exact value stored in the database, right side = what participants see on screen. The job areas are already stored with readable names, so no overrides are needed. Add an entry here only if you ever want to show a different label for one.

instructions.title: About this study
instructions.lead: Here is what the rest of the study involves before you agree to take part.
whatYouDo: Enter your Prolific ID so we can credit you.
Answer two quick questions about your assigned role.

Read **16 emails** one at a time. They vary in who sends them (a **senior** colleague, or a **peer or junior** one), whether the task is asked **with a priority** or **with no rush**, and what is said about it (a **reward** for doing it, or a **consequence** for not).
Rate how realistic each one is and say why. If something feels off, mark the exact part and rewrite it as you would expect.
instructions.compensation: There are no right or wrong answers. It takes about **25 minutes** and pays **£3.75**.
instructions.consentText: Your responses are collected for academic research on how people judge the realism of workplace emails. Participation is voluntary and you may stop at any time. Responses are stored against your Prolific ID, kept confidential, and used only for research.
instructions.consentCheckbox: I have read the above and I consent to take part in this study.

clusterSelect.title: Email realism validation
clusterSelect.lead: Thank you for taking part. In this short study you will take on a work role from an area close to your own, and read a set of everyday emails written as if they landed in that role's inbox. We want your honest judgement of whether each email feels realistic for that role.
clusterSelect.selectPrompt: Which job area is closest to you?
clusterSelect.selectHelp: Pick the single area that best matches your own work experience. The emails you review will be written for a role in that area.

prolific.title: Your Prolific ID
prolific.lead: Please enter your Prolific ID exactly as it appears in your Prolific account.
prolific.invalidHint: That does not look like a Prolific ID. Check for spaces or missing characters.

role.title: Your job role for this task
role.lead: For the rest of the study, **imagine** you are the person described below, working inside a **large company** with many departments (finance, IT, HR, sales, operations, procurement and more). You receive **work-related emails** from them.
role.fitText: We will ask your **expert opinion** on the emails a **{role}** typically receives at work. You do not need to have held this exact job, but it should be familiar enough that you can judge which of its emails are realistic.
role.fitQuestion: Do you feel you know this kind of role well enough to judge which emails are realistic for it?
role.fitConfirmButton: Yes, this is familiar to me, continue
role.fitDeclineButton: No, I do not know this kind of role well
role.exitTitle: This study may not be a good fit
role.exitText: Thank you for your honesty. This study needs people who are familiar with the day-to-day emails of the role shown, so your responses would not be the right fit here. No data has been saved and you have not consented to anything.
role.exitProlificNote: Please return your submission on Prolific so it can be offered to someone else.
role.exitProlificButton: Take me back to my Prolific

checks.heading: Quick checks
checks.intro: Two quick questions to confirm the role is clear. The emails only make sense once you are picturing yourself in it.
checks._note: For each question, 'answer' is the number of the correct option, counting the options from the top starting at 1. Keep the correct option and 'answer' in sync if you reorder them.

questions.prompt: For the rest of this study, who should you imagine you are?
options: Myself, in my own current job
A customer writing in to the company
A job applicant hoping to be hired

questions.prompt: When you rate each email, what are you mainly judging?
options: Whether the email has spelling or grammar mistakes
Whether I personally like the sender
Whether a {role} would realistically receive an email like this
Whether the email is polite enough

checks.error: Not quite. Think again about your role, then choose again.

recapPoints: You will read **16 emails**, one per page, each addressed to you as a **{role}**.
For each, first rate **how realistic** it is for your role.
If **anything feels off**, tell us what, then use the **Edit** buttons to rewrite just those parts.
You can add an optional comment before the next email.
recap.nameHeading: Pick a name for the emails
recap.nameLead: So the emails read naturally, each will greet you by a first name you choose (for example, *Dear Alex,*).
recap.nameWarning: Please do **not** use your own real name.
recap.nameFieldLabel: First name to use in the emails
recap.nameInvalidHint: Please enter a single first name using letters only.
recap.nameConfirmCheckbox: I confirm this is not my own real name.

_note: Shown once, right after the recap and before the 16 emails. Participants give their general expectation for eight everyday email situations BEFORE they have seen any of our example emails, so these are their un-primed judgments. The eight items are the 2x2x2 combinations of sender (more senior vs peer or junior), urgency (right away vs no rush) and framing (a problem to avoid vs a benefit to gain). Keep the wording plain and do NOT name those three factors on screen. The order is shuffled per participant automatically. 'key' is stored in the data and must not be changed; edit only the 'text'.
priorJudgments.lead: Before you read any of the emails, we would like your **general expectation**. Below are eight everyday situations. For each, tell us how believable it is that you, as a **{role}**, would receive an email like that at work. There are no right answers, and you have not seen our emails yet, so just go with your honest sense.
priorJudgments.requiredHint: Please rate all eight situations before you continue.

items.text: A **senior** colleague asks you to do a task **with a priority**, mentioning a **potential consequence** if you do not do it.
A **senior** colleague asks you to do a task **with a priority**, mentioning a **reward** if you do it.
A **senior** colleague asks you to do a task **with no rush**, mentioning a **potential consequence** if you do not do it.
A **senior** colleague asks you to do a task **with no rush**, mentioning a **reward** if you do it.
A **peer or junior** colleague asks you to do a task **with a priority**, mentioning a **potential consequence** if you do not do it.
A **peer or junior** colleague asks you to do a task **with a priority**, mentioning a **reward** if you do it.
A **peer or junior** colleague asks you to do a task **with no rush**, mentioning a **potential consequence** if you do not do it.
A **peer or junior** colleague asks you to do a task **with no rush**, mentioning a **reward** if you do it.

email.conditionsTitle: What to expect in this email
email.conditionsSentence: This email is from {dir}. It asks you to do a task {urg}, and mentions {frame}.
email.conditionsLead: Read it as if it just arrived in your inbox, then answer the questions below.

conditions._note: The summary line at the top of each email is built from these parts and is worded to match the eight situations on the 'Before you begin reading' page. The template is conditionsSentence above: 'This email is from {dir}. It asks you to do a task {urg}, and mentions {frame}.' Keep the **bold** on the sender, the urgency phrase and the reward/consequence.

frame.threat: a **potential consequence** if you do not do it

email.questionsIntro: Now please answer the questions below about the email above.
email.q1Heading: 1. Is this email realistic for your role?
email.q1Sub: As a {role}, would an email like this, from this kind of sender, put this way, realistically land in your inbox? Judge the sender, the urgency, and the framing **as a combination**, not each on its own.
email.q1Required: Please choose a rating to continue.
email.q2Heading: 2. In a sentence or two, why did you give that rating?
email.q2Sub: Tell us what made it feel realistic or not for a **{role}**. This helps us understand your rating.
email.q2Placeholder: For example: someone at my level would never be asked this directly by a sender like a CEO.
email.q2Required: Please add a short explanation for your rating to continue.
email.q3Heading: 3. Would you change anything to make it more realistic for your role?
email.q3Sub: Optional. If anything felt unrealistic for a **{role}**, feel free to edit the email directly to show what you would change. You can also add a short note below. If it all seems realistic, you can leave it as it is.
email.q3EditHint: You can now edit the subject and the message text above. The sender, the link, and the signature stay as they are. Click Done editing when you have finished.
email.q3NotePlaceholder: For example: a request like this would come from my line manager, not the CEO, and there would be no same-day deadline.

done.title: Thank you
done.lead: Your responses for all 16 emails have been recorded. You can now return to Prolific to complete your submission.

roles._note: Each key is the job area as stored in the database. 'title' is the assigned role name and 'description' is the short blurb shown on the role and recap pages.

Finance.title: Financial Accountant
Finance.description: Handles company accounts, ledgers, reconciliations and financial reporting.

IT Support.title: IT Support Analyst
IT Support.description: Front-line IT helpdesk, handling requests from managers, IT colleagues and internal staff.

HR.title: Recruitment Coordinator
HR.description: Coordinates hiring end to end, working with managers and HR and payroll colleagues.

Sales.title: Sales Executive
Sales.description: Manages business customer accounts, quotes, deals and follow-ups.

Operations.title: Operations Manager
Operations.description: Runs day-to-day business operations, working with operations and facilities colleagues.

Customer Service.title: Customer Support Specialist
Customer Service.description: Handles corporate customer accounts, orders and complaints.

Marketing.title: Marketing Manager
Marketing.description: Owns marketing campaigns and budgets, working with sales, finance and marketing colleagues.

Procurement.title: Procurement Specialist
Procurement.description: Handles company purchasing, supplier quotes, orders and contracts.

Administrative Support.title: Executive Assistant
Administrative Support.description: Supports senior leadership with scheduling, travel, correspondence and office coordination.

Compliance.title: Compliance Officer
Compliance.description: Handles KYC/CDD checks, transaction-monitoring alerts and case escalations.
